redeem.meta.description.content: You've received a Bitcoin Lightning voucher. Claim it instantly with a Lightning wallet.

redeem.meta.ogDescription.content: Someone sent you a Lightning voucher. Claim it instantly with a Bitcoin wallet — no account needed.

redeem.modal.lnaddr.body.text: Enter a Lightning address or LNURL pay code to receive the sats directly to your wallet.

redeem.modal.sats.whatBody.text: Satoshis ("sats") are the smallest unit of Bitcoin — like cents to a rand. There are 100 million sats in one Bitcoin. You can save them, spend them, or send them to anyone in the world instantly.

redeem.modal.sats.li1.html: Get a Bitcoin wallet — download Blink (recommended for beginners) from the App Store or Play Store.

redeem.modal.sats.li2.html: Tap "Show Voucher" on this page to show the QR code.

redeem.modal.sats.li3.html: Scan or tap the QR code in your wallet app to receive the sats.

redeem.modal.sats.doTitle.text: What can I do with them?

redeem.modal.sats.doBody.text: Spend at thousands of merchants, pay bills, send to friends, or hold as savings. See the wallet guide below for more details.

redeem.serverNotice.suffix.text: added to your server list in Settings.

redeem.state.invalidDesc.text: Check the link or contact the sender.

redeem.state.expiredDesc.text: This voucher expired before it was redeemed. Ask the sender for a new one.

redeem.state.redeemedDesc.text: This voucher has already been redeemed.

redeem.state.unfundedDesc.text: This voucher hasn't been loaded with Bitcoin yet. Come back once the sender has loaded it.

redeem.lnaddr.emptyError.text: Please enter a Lightning address or LNURL pay code.

redeem.lnaddr.genericError.text: Something went wrong. Please try again.

redeem.faq.blink.answer.html:
✅ Great for beginners. Low setup, fast to start. Your phone number is your backup.

Steps to get started

  1. Download blink.sv From the Play Store, GitHub, or App Store.
    Play Store APK App Store
  2. Open the app Tap "Create new account" -> "Accept"
  3. Enter your phone number (optional) "SMS" -> "Use SMS" -> Enter number -> Tap "Send via SMS". This is your backup. Confirm the 6 digit code.
  4. Come back here Tap the QR code above. This will open Blink and allow you to redeem the bitcoin ⚡

redeem.faq.spend.title.text: Where can I spend Bitcoin in South Africa?

redeem.faq.spend.lead.text: Use Bitcoin everywhere — local markets, big stores, gift cards, and instant global payments.

redeem.faq.spend.circlesBody.html:
Entire communities where you can live on Bitcoin: Witsand, Mossel Bay (bitcoinekasi.com), Plettenberg Bay (bitcoinplett.cx), De Rust, Sedgefield, Stellenbosch, and Knysna.
All have regular local meetups.

redeem.faq.spend.mapBody.html: BTCMap.org and the Money Badger map show thousands of Bitcoin-accepting businesses across South Africa.

redeem.faq.spend.zapperBody.html:
Pay at any store that accepts Zapper or Scan to Pay — including
                  Pick & Pay, Dischem, Clicks, Engen, Shell, Total, Wimpy and many more.

redeem.faq.spend.giftBody.html: Buy gift cards for hundreds of global brands at Bitrefill.com — Netflix, Amazon, Uber, Airtime top-ups and more.

redeem.faq.spend.meetupsBody.html:
Active communities in Cape Town, Pretoria, and Johannesburg.
                  Find events and dates on Twitter/X — search for local Bitcoin groups in your city.

redeem.faq.spend.instantBody.text: Lightning payments settle in under a second, worldwide, for near-zero fees.

redeem.info.buying.text: 🟠 Buying and Selling Rands for Bitcoin

redeem.faq.buyZa.q.text: Where can I buy Bitcoin in South Africa?

redeem.faq.buyZa.a.html: You can buy Bitcoin using services like 54052.co.za, which allow you to pay via EFT and receive Bitcoin directly to your wallet. Choose a service that is simple, reliable, and lets you withdraw to your own wallet.

redeem.faq.bankZa.q.text: How do I send Bitcoin to a South African bank account?

redeem.faq.bankZa.a.html: You can use 54052.co.za to convert Bitcoin to rands and send it directly to a South African bank account. It’s simple to use, Bitcoin-focused, and designed for fast, low-fee payouts.

redeem.faq.privacy.q.text: Why should I use my own wallet and care about privacy?

redeem.faq.privacy.a.html:
When your Bitcoin is on an exchange, you don’t control it — the exchange does. Accounts can be frozen, restricted, or lost due to hacks or other issues.

Most exchanges also require KYC (identity verification), which links your real identity to your Bitcoin activity. This creates a permanent record of your transactions and reduces your financial privacy.

"Not your keys, not your coins." Using your own wallet gives you full control over your money and helps protect your privacy. Treat it like cash — once you own it, keep it with you.

redeem.faq.crypto.q.text: What is the difference between Bitcoin and crypto?

redeem.faq.crypto.a.html:
Bitcoin is decentralized money — no founders, no company, no central control. The rules are fixed and enforced by a global network. It was designed to remove trust and give individuals full control over their money.

"Crypto" is everything else — projects with founders, teams, and insiders who can change the rules, influence supply, and benefit early. This makes them easy to manipulate and unsuitable as money.

Most crypto projects don’t solve the core problem of fiat — control. They simply move that control from governments to companies or small groups.

The technologies may be interesting, but they are open source and can be built on Bitcoin without needing separate tokens. The tokens themselves are usually unnecessary and exist to enrich insiders.

Money is driven by network effects — people converge on one standard. Our view is simple: Bitcoin is money. Crypto is not. To keep our community safe, we focus on Bitcoin only and avoid crypto entirely.

redeem.faq.lightning.q.text: Why does this platform only support Lightning?

redeem.faq.lightning.a.html:
Lightning is Bitcoin’s payment layer. It allows instant transactions, near-zero fees, and works globally in seconds.

On-chain Bitcoin is slower and more expensive — better suited for saving. Lightning makes Bitcoin practical for everyday payments like sending small amounts or paying in shops.

This platform uses Lightning to make sending Bitcoin simple, fast, and accessible to anyone.

redeem.back.qrHint.text: Tap or Scan the QR code to claim
